avenues.name: Superconducting
avenues.thesis: Most mature at scale; Google's Willow showed below-threshold error correction. Heavy surface-code overhead and chip-to-chip interconnect are the scaling frontier.
avenues.signpost: up if modular interconnect and logical scaling keep working; down if surface-code overhead proves intractable

avenues.name: Trapped ion
avenues.thesis: Highest demonstrated gate quality and the best (lowest) physical-to-logical ratio; scaling qubit count is the open question.
avenues.signpost: up if a chip-based ion system scales past hundreds of qubits with fidelity intact

avenues.name: Neutral atom
avenues.thesis: Fastest-rising; leads in error-corrected qubit count with cheap identical atoms and software-defined connectivity; a younger control stack.
avenues.signpost: up if sustained large logical-qubit operation at higher fidelity is demonstrated

avenues.name: Silicon spin
avenues.thesis: The quantum-dot computing approach: best manufacturing/scaling story and freshly proven quality on a foundry line, but fewest qubits and no logical qubit yet.
avenues.signpost: up if a first credible logical qubit is built on silicon spin

avenues.name: Photonic
avenues.thesis: Room-temperature and natively networked, but gated almost entirely by unsolved optical loss and probabilistic gates.
avenues.signpost: up if optical loss drops below the fault-tolerance threshold with a logical qubit demonstrated

avenues.name: III-V quantum dots
avenues.thesis: Optically-active compound-semiconductor dots (~10-12%): best-in-class photon sources and spin-photon interfaces — a component for photonic/network architectures more than a self-contained processor.
avenues.signpost: up if a credible scalable processor architecture built on III-V quantum dots appears (not merely an improved photon-source benchmark)

avenues.name: Topological
avenues.thesis: A bet on an unproven physics claim — the existence of the qubit itself is contested; intrinsic hardware-level error protection if it is real.
avenues.signpost: independent reproduction of an accepted topological qubit; could collapse toward 0 or jump materially

avenues.name: NV centers
avenues.thesis: Room-temperature diamond defect, superb for sensing/networking; very hard to scale into large, connected general-purpose processors.
avenues.signpost: up if a credible multi-qubit error-corrected NV processor architecture with a real scaling path appears (not another sensing/networking milestone)

checks._comment: Single source of truth for the consistency-check RULES. Both index.html's console and verification/verify_numbers.py read these, so a threshold can never drift between the page and the verifier. Change a rule HERE and both sides enforce it on next run. (The check LOGIC is still written in each language — see AUTHORING.md — but the rules it enforces live only here.)
